title: Raja Enbar Mandhiri Enbar Lyrics Bhuvana Oru Kelvi Kuri

Raja enbar mandhiri enbar
raajiyam illai aala
oru raaniyum illai vazha
oru uravum illai
adhil pirivum illai

Raja enbar mandhiri enbar
raajiyam illai aala

vaithiyam illai
ulagil enakku
oru vazhi illai.

Raja enbar mandhiri enbar
raajiyam illai aala
oru raaniyum illai vazha

nilavukku vaanam undu

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

The song reflects themes of longing and the absence of connection, evoking a sense of nostalgia.

In which movie does this song appear?

This song is featured in the movie Bhuvana Oru Kelvi Kuri.

Who are the singers of this song?

The song is sung by the renowned artists S.Janaki and S.P.Balasubramaniam.

Who composed the music for this song?

The music for this song was composed by the legendary Ilaiyaraaja.

When was this song released?

The song was released in the year 1977.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Raja Enbar Mandhiri Enbar, the poignant lyrics encapsulate the essence of longing and the absence of meaningful connections. The song unfolds within the narrative of Bhuvana Oru Kelvi Kuri, where emotions run deep and resonate with the audience. With its melodious composition by Ilaiyaraaja and the soulful rendition by S.Janaki and S.P.Balasubramaniam, it evokes a sense of nostalgia that transcends time. This classic piece remains a cherished gem in Tamil cinema, inviting listeners to reflect on their own experiences of love and loss.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Raja Enbar Mandhiri Enbar is a timeless classic that resonates with the soul, showcasing the enchanting voices of S.Janaki and S.P.Balasubramaniam. The song, composed by the legendary Ilaiyaraaja, captures a profound sense of nostalgia and emotion. Dive into the lyrical beauty of Bhuvana Oru Kelvi Kuri and experience the magic of old Tamil cinema.
stanzaMeanings: There is no kingdom, nor a queen; there are no relationships, and no separations in it.
I swing in the world every day.
There is no kingdom, nor a queen; there is no way to live.
There is no joy in the heart, no peace in the mind, no satisfaction in desires, and no chance to experience.
There is no cure for madness, and I have no way in this world.
There is no kingdom, nor a queen; there is no way to live.
The moon has a sky, the flower has a place to live, and what do I have?
Why was I created? I have no peace in my mind.
There is a kingdom for you, and there is a prince for you.
There are good relationships, and there is love in them; I swing in the world every day.
I see the divine in you, and I worship you every day; I stand for the moon and the relationship it brings.
There is confusion and chaos in the world, and you have a history.
There is a kingdom for you, and there is a prince for you.
There are good relationships, and there is love in them; I swing in the world every day.
